volumeTitle: Birds. Lysistrata. Women at the Thesmophoria
label: Birds (Section 828 - Section 961)

sections.label: Section 828
sections.original: τί δʼ οὐκ Ἀθηναίαν ἐῶμεν Πολιάδα ;
sections.translation: Why not choose Athena Polias ?

sections.label: Section 829
sections.original: καὶ πῶς ἂν ἔτι γένοιτʼ ἂν εὔτακτος πόλις ,
sections.translation: Oh ! what a well-ordered town it would be

sections.label: Section 830
sections.original: ὅπου θεὸς γυνὴ γεγονυῖα πανοπλίαν
sections.translation: to have a female deity armed from head to foot , while Clisthenes was spinning !

sections.label: Section 832
sections.original: τίς δαὶ καθέξει τῆς πόλεως τὸ Πελαργικόν ;
sections.translation: Who then shall guard the Pelargicon ?

sections.label: Section 833
sections.original: ὄρνις ἀφʼ ἡμῶν τοῦ γένους τοῦ Περσικοῦ ,
sections.translation: A bird of Persian strain , who is everywhere proclaimed to be the bravest of all ,

sections.label: Section 835
sections.original: Ἄρεως νεοττός .
sections.translation: a true chick of Ares .

sections.label: Section 835b
sections.original: ὦ νεοττὲ δέσποτα ·
sections.translation: Oh ! noble chick !

sections.label: Section 837
sections.original: ἄγε νυν σὺ μὲν βάδιζε πρὸς τὸν ἀέρα
sections.translation: Because he is a god well suited to live on the rocks . Come ! into the air with you to help the workers who are building the wall ; carry up rubble , strip yourself to mix the mortar ,

sections.label: Section 840
sections.original: λεκάνην ἀνένεγκε , κατάπεσʼ ἀπὸ τῆς κλίμακος ,
sections.translation: take up the hod , tumble down the ladder , if you like , post sentinels , keep the fire smouldering beneath the ashes , go round the walls , bell in hand , and go to sleep up there yourself ; then despatch two heralds , one to the gods above , the other to mankind on earth

sections.label: Section 845
sections.original: κἀκεῖθεν αὖθις παρʼ ἐμέ .
sections.translation: and come back here .

sections.label: Section 845b
sections.original: σὺ δέ γʼ αὐτοῦ μένων
sections.translation: As for yourself , remain here , and may the plague take you for a troublesome fellow ! He departs .

sections.label: Section 846b
sections.original: ἴθʼ ὦγάθʼ οἷ πέμπω σʼ ἐγώ .
sections.translation: Go , friend , go where I send you , for without you my orders cannot be obeyed . For myself , I want to sacrifice to the new god , and I am going to summon the priest who must preside at the ceremony .

sections.label: Section 850
sections.original: παῖ παῖ , τὸ κανοῦν αἴρεσθε καὶ τὴν χέρνιβα .
sections.translation: Slaves ! slaves ! bring forward the basket and the lustral water .

sections.label: Section 1.851
sections.original: ὁμορροθῶ , συνθέλω ,
sections.translation: I do as you do , and I wish as you wish , and I implore you to address powerful and solemn prayers to the gods , and in addition

sections.label: Section 1.855
sections.original: ἅμα δὲ προσέτι χάριτος ἕνεκα προβάτιόν τι θύειν .
sections.translation: to immolate a sheep as a token of our gratitude . Let us sing the Pythian chant in honor of the god , and let Chaeris accompany our voices .

sections.label: Section 1.859
sections.original: παῦσαι σὺ φυσῶν . Ἡράκλεις τουτὶ τί ἦν ;
sections.translation: Enough ! but , by Heracles ! what is this ?

sections.label: Section 1.860
sections.original: τουτὶ μὰ Δίʼ ἐγὼ πολλὰ δὴ καὶ δείνʼ ἰδὼν
sections.translation: Great gods ! I have seen many prodigious things , but I never saw a muzzled raven . The Priest arrives . Priest ! it ' s high time ! Sacrifice to the new gods .

sections.label: Section 1.864
sections.original: δράσω τάδʼ . ἀλλὰ ποῦ ʼστιν ὁ τὸ κανοῦν ἔχων ;
sections.translation: I begin , but where is the man with the basket ? Pray to the Hestia of the birds , to the kite , who presides over the hearth , and to all the god and goddess-birds who dwell in Olympus . . .

sections.label: Section 1.866
sections.original: ὦ Σουνιέρακε χαῖρʼ ἄναξ Πελαργικέ .
sections.translation: Oh ! Hawk , the sacred guardian of Sunium , oh , god of the storks !

sections.label: Section 1.867
sections.original: καὶ κύκνῳ Πυθίῳ καὶ Δηλίῳ καὶ Λητοῖ Ὀρτυγομήτρᾳ καὶ Ἀρτέμιδι Ἀκαλανθίδι —
sections.translation: . . . to the swan of Delos , to Leto the mother of the quails , and to Artemis , the goldfinch . . .

sections.label: Section 1.868
sections.original: οὐκέτι Κολαινὶς ἀλλʼ Ἀκαλανθὶς Ἄρτεμις .
sections.translation: It ' s no longer Artemis Colaenis , but Artemis the goldfinch .

sections.label: Section 1.876
sections.original: καὶ φρυγίλῳ Σαβαζίῳ καὶ στρούθῳ μεγάλῃ μητρὶ θεῶν καὶ ἀνθρώπων —
sections.translation: . . . to Bacchus , the finch and Cybele , the ostrich and mother of the gods and mankind . . .

sections.label: Section 1.877
sections.original: δέσποινα Κυβέλη , στροῦθε , μῆτερ Κλεοκρίτου .
sections.translation: Oh ! sovereign ostrich Cybele , mother of Cleocritus !

sections.label: Section 1.878
sections.original: διδόναι Νεφελοκοκκυγιεῦσιν ὑγιείαν καὶ σωτηρίαν αὐτοῖσι καὶ Χίοισι —
sections.translation: . . . to grant health and safety to the Nephelococcygians as well as to the dwellers in Chios . . .

sections.label: Section 1.880
sections.original: Χίοισιν ἥσθην πανταχοῦ προσκειμένοις .
sections.translation: The dwellers in Chios ! Ah ! I am delighted they should be thus mentioned on all occasions .

sections.label: Section 1.881
sections.original: καὶ ἥρωσιν ὄρνισι καὶ ἡρώων παισί , πορφυρίωνι καὶ
sections.translation: . . . to the heroes , the birds , to the sons of heroes , to the porphyrion , the pelican , the spoon-bill , the redbreast , the grouse , the peacock , the horned-owl , the teal , the bittern , the heron , the stormy petrel , the fig-pecker , the titmouse . . .

sections.label: Section 1.889
sections.original: παῦʼ ἐς κόρακας , παῦσαι καλῶν . ἰοὺ ἰού ,
sections.translation: Stop ! stop ! you drive me crazy with your endless list . Why , wretch ,

sections.label: Section 1.890
sections.original: ἐπὶ ποῖον ὦ κακόδαιμον ἱερεῖον καλεῖς
sections.translation: to what sacred feast are you inviting the vultures and the sea-eagles ? Don ' t you see that a single kite could easily carry off the lot at once ? Begone , you and your fillets and all ; I shall know how to complete the sacrifice by myself .

sections.label: Section 1.895
sections.original: εἶτʼ αὖθις αὖ τἄρα σοι
sections.translation: It is imperative that I sing another sacred chant for the rite of the lustral water , and that I invoke

sections.label: Section 1.900
sections.original: μάκαρας , ἕνα τινὰ μόνον , εἴπερ ἱκανὸν ἕξετʼ ὄψον .
sections.translation: the immortals , or at least one of them , provided always that you have some suitable food to offer him ; from what I see here , in the shape of gifts , there is naught whatever but horn and hair .

sections.label: Section 1.903
sections.original: θύοντες εὐξώμεσθα τοῖς πτερίνοις θεοῖς .
sections.translation: Let us address our sacrifices and our prayers to the winged gods .

sections.label: Section 904
sections.original: Νεφελοκοκκυγίαν τὰν εὐδαίμονα
sections.translation: Oh , Muse ! celebrate happy Nephelococcygia in your hymns .

sections.label: Section 906
sections.original: τουτὶ τὸ πρᾶγμα ποδαπόν ; εἰπέ μοι τίς εἶ ;
sections.translation: What have we here ? Where did you come from , tell me ? Who are you ?

sections.label: Section 907
sections.original: ἐγὼ μελιγλώσσων ἐπέων ἱεὶς ἀοιδὰν
sections.translation: I am he whose language is sweeter than honey , the zealous slave of the Muses ,

sections.label: Section 910
sections.original: κατὰ τὸν Ὅμηρον .
sections.translation: as Homer has it .

sections.label: Section 911
sections.original: ἔπειτα δῆτα δοῦλος ὢν κόμην ἔχεις ;
sections.translation: You a slave ! and yet you wear your hair long ?

sections.label: Section 912
sections.original: οὔκ , ἀλλὰ πάντες ἐσμὲν οἱ διδάσκαλοι
sections.translation: No , but the fact is all we poets are the assiduous slaves of the Muses , according to Homer .

sections.label: Section 915
sections.original: οὐκ ἐτὸς ὀτρηρὸν καὶ τὸ ληδάριον ἔχεις .
sections.translation: In truth your little cloak is quite holy too through zeal ! But , poet , what ill wind drove you here ?

sections.label: Section 917
sections.original: μέλη πεποίηκʼ ἐς τὰς Νεφελοκοκκυγίας
sections.translation: I have composed verses in honor of your Nephelococcygia , a host of splendid dithyrambs and parthenia worthy of Simonides himself .

sections.label: Section 920
sections.original: ταυτὶ σὺ πότʼ ἐποίησας ; ἀπὸ ποίου χρόνου ;
sections.translation: And when did you compose them ? How long since ?

sections.label: Section 921
sections.original: πάλαι πάλαι δὴ τήνδʼ ἐγὼ κλῄζω πόλιν .
sections.translation: Oh ! ' tis long , aye , very long , that I have sung in honor of this city .

sections.label: Section 922
sections.original: οὐκ ἄρτι θύω τὴν δεκάτην ταύτης ἐγώ ,
sections.translation: But I am only celebrating its foundation with this sacrifice ; I have only just named it , as is done with little babies .

sections.label: Section 924
sections.original: ἀλλά τις ὠκεῖα Μουσάων φάτις
sections.translation: Just as the chargers fly with the speed of the wind , so does the voice of the Muses take its flight . Oh ! thou noble founder of the town of Aetna thou , whose name recalls the holy sacrifices ,

sections.label: Section 930
sections.original: πρόφρων δόμεν ἐμὶν τείν .
sections.translation: make us such gift as thy generous heart shall suggest . He puts out his hand .

sections.label: Section 931
sections.original: τουτὶ παρέξει τὸ κακὸν ἡμῖν πράγματα ,
sections.translation: He will drive us silly if we do not get rid of him by some present . To the Priest ' s acolyte . Here ! you , who have a fur as well as your tunic , take it off and give it to this clever poet . Come ,

sections.label: Section 935
sections.original: ἔχε τὴν σπολάδα · πάντως δέ μοι ῥιγῶν δοκεῖς .
sections.translation: take this fur ; you look to me to be shivering with cold .

sections.label: Section 936
sections.original: τόδε μὲν οὐκ ἀέκουσα φίλα
sections.translation: My Muse will gladly accept this gift ; but engrave these verses of Pindar ' s on your mind .

sections.label: Section 940
sections.original: ἅνθρωπος ἡμῶν οὐκ ἀπαλλαχθήσεται .
sections.translation: Oh ! what a pest ! It ' s impossible then to get rid of him !

sections.label: Section 941
sections.original: νομάδεσσι γὰρ ἐν Σκύθαις
sections.translation: Straton wanders among the Scythian nomads , but has no linen garment . He is sad at only wearing an animal ' s pelt and no tunic .

sections.label: Section 945
sections.original: ξύνες ὅ τοι λέγω .
sections.translation: Do you get what I mean ?

sections.label: Section 946
sections.original: ξυνῆχʼ ὅτι βούλει τὸν χιτωνίσκον λαβεῖν .
sections.translation: I understand that you want me to offer you a tunic . Hi ! you To the acolyte . take off yours ; we must help the poet . . . . Come , you , take it and get out .

sections.label: Section 948b
sections.original: ἀπέρχομαι ,
sections.translation: I am going , and these are the verses that I address to this city :

sections.label: Section 950
sections.original: κλῇσον ὦ χρυσόθρονε τὰν τρομερὰν κρυεράν ·
sections.translation: Phoebus of the golden throne , celebrate this shivery , freezing city ; I have travelled through fruitful and snow-covered plains . Tralala ! Tralala ! He departs .

sections.label: Section 952
sections.original: νὴ τὸν Δίʼ ἀλλʼ ἤδη πέφευγας ταυταγὶ
sections.translation: What are you chanting us

sections.label: Section 955
sections.original: τὰ κρυερὰ τονδὶ τὸν χιτωνίσκον λαβών .
sections.translation: about frosts ? Thanks to the tunic , you no longer fear them . Ah ! by Zeus ! I could not have believed this cursed fellow could so soon have learnt the way to our city . To a slave . Come , take the lustral water and circle the altar . Let all keep silence !

sections.label: Section 959b
sections.original: μὴ κατάρξῃ τοῦ τράγου .
sections.translation: Let not the goat be sacrificed .

sections.label: Section 960
sections.original: σὺ δʼ εἶ τίς ;
sections.translation: Who are you ?

sections.label: Section 960b
sections.original: ὅστις ; χρησμολόγος .
sections.translation: Who am I ? An oracle-monger .

sections.label: Section 960c
sections.original: οἴμωζέ νυν .
sections.translation: Get out !

sections.label: Section 961
sections.original: ὦ δαιμόνιε τὰ θεῖα μὴ φαύλως φέρε ·
sections.translation: Wretched man , insult not sacred things . For there is an oracle of Bacis , which exactly applies to Nephelococcygia .
